verdict.summary: Guide is broadly useful, but contains several material errors on D4 funds/post-study residence/citizenship counting, DGES scholarships, FCT grant amounts, medicine/integrated degrees, and some misleading admissions/cost statements.

issues.current: Документы: acceptance letter, proof of funds (>€7000/год), медстраховка, apartment
issues.problem: The proof-of-means figure is outdated/misleading. For 2026 the Portuguese minimum wage is €920/month, and subsistence calculations for residence visas are normally tied to the minimum wage, not a fixed €7,000/year. €7,000 is below 12×SMN 2026 (€11,040).
issues.suggestion: Write: "proof of means: ориентируйтесь минимум на 12×SMN в год для основного заявителя — в 2026 это €11,040; консульство/VFS может принимать стипендию, банковский баланс, спонсорство и/или prepaid accommodation depending on post."
issues.source: Decreto-Lei 112/2025 (SMN 2026 €920); Portaria 1563/2007 on means of subsistence

issues.current: После окончания: Procuro-trabalho D-виза (120 дней на поиск работы)
issues.problem: This confuses the external job-seeker visa with the post-study residence route. The 120-day job-seeker visa is for applying from abroad and is extendable once; graduates already legally resident in Portugal normally use a residence permit/renewal route to look for work or start a business, commonly up to 12 months under the student/researcher mobility framework.
issues.suggestion: Write: "После окончания: можно запросить autorização de residência para procura de trabalho ou criação de empresa после завершения обучения — обычно до 12 месяцев; это не та же процедура, что visto para procura de trabalho на 120+60 дней для въезда из-за границы."
issues.source: Lei 23/2007, art. 122 and AIMA guidance on residence permits for students/researchers

issues.current: Время учёбы ЗАСЧИТЫВАЕТСЯ в стаж для гражданства (но с коэффициентом 0.5 — т.е. 2 года учёбы = 1 год стажа)
issues.problem: Portugal does not have a general 'student residence counts at 0.5' rule for nationality. Lawful temporary residence time is counted normally for the residence-period requirement. Also, as of April 2026 the new nationality law was approved on 01.04.2026 but was awaiting promulgation, so any statement about future residence counting needs a caveat.
issues.suggestion: Write: "По действующему праву нет коэффициента 0.5: годы с действующим título de residência студента обычно считаются как обычное легальное проживание для натурализации. Важно: новая редакция закона о гражданстве была одобрена 01.04.2026 и ждёт промульгации; правила могут измениться после вступления в силу."
issues.source: Lei da Nacionalidade, Lei 37/81, art. 6; new nationality law approved 01.04.2026 awaiting promulgation

issues.current: **Mestrado Integrado**: 5 лет, 300 ECTS — для медицины, архитектуры, инженерии; единый курс вместо bac+mag
issues.problem: Medicine in Portugal is normally an integrated master of 6 years / 360 ECTS, not 5 years / 300 ECTS. Architecture is typically 300 ECTS; engineering integrated masters have been reduced/changed in many institutions after legal reforms and should not be presented as the default.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Mestrado Integrado: для некоторых регулируемых профессий. Medicina — обычно 6 лет / 360 ECTS; Arquitetura — часто 5 лет / 300 ECTS; в Engenharia многие antigos mestrados integrados были reorganized into licenciatura + mestrado, check each course in DGES."
issues.source: DGES course registry; A3ES accreditation listings

issues.current: Граждане ЕС / ЕЭЗ / Швейцарии + резиденты PT
issues.problem: The eligibility split is oversimplified. Not every Portuguese resident can automatically use the national access route instead of the international student competition. Under the international student statute, non-EU nationals are excluded from 'international student' status only in specific cases, including legal residence in Portugal for more than two uninterrupted years on 1 January of the application year, excluding residence time based solely on study, or where equal-rights status applies.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Граждане PT/ЕС/ЕЭЗ/Швейцарии и некоторые non-EU резиденты: CNA applies if you are not considered an 'estudante internacional' — e.g. legal residence in Portugal for >2 uninterrupted years by 1 January, not only on a student permit, or equal-rights status."
issues.source: Decreto-Lei 36/2014, Estatuto do Estudante Internacional

issues.current: Требования: диплом средней школы, подходящий для high ed в стране происхождения + certificado de equivalência (1-3 мес на получение через DGES)
issues.problem: Secondary-school equivalence is not obtained through DGES. DGES handles higher-education access information and recognition of foreign higher degrees, but equivalence/recognition of foreign basic/secondary education is handled through schools/DGE processes. Also, universities may accept foreign qualifications directly under their CEIE rules without a generic 'DGES equivalência' in every case.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Требования зависят от университета: foreign secondary qualification giving access to higher education in the issuing country; sometimes equivalência/reconhecimento do ensino secundário via Portuguese secondary school/DGE process, plus translated/legalized documents. Check the university CEIE edital."
issues.source: DGE guidance on equivalence of foreign basic/secondary education; DGES international student access rules

issues.current: ⚠️ Некоторые факультеты медицины, ветеринарии имеют квоту только для PT/ЕС — не-ЕС не могут поступить
issues.problem: This is imprecise. The public international-student special competition generally does not apply to integrated master degrees in medicine. Veterinary medicine is not categorically excluded in the same way; restrictions depend on the institution/course and numerus clausus.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Medicina: public CEIE generally does not apply to mestrado integrado em medicina, so non-EU applicants usually cannot use the international-student quota for public medicine. Veterinária and other regulated courses: check each edital; restrictions are course-specific, not a blanket national ban."
issues.source: Decreto-Lei 36/2014, art. 3

issues.current: Process: regresso ao ensino superior
issues.problem: This names the wrong access regime for transferring from another university/course. 'Regresso' is generally for former students returning to a course/institution they previously attended. Transfer/change is 'mudança de par instituição/curso'.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Process: mudança de par instituição/curso for transfer/change of institution/course; regresso applies to returning to a course where you were previously enrolled."
issues.source: DGES regimes for mudança de par instituição/curso and reingresso

issues.problem: For public licenciatura and integrated master programmes covered by the national tuition cap, the annual tuition for national/EU-equivalent students is generally capped around €697, not €1,400. €1,400 may occur in other contexts but is misleading as a bachelor public-university range for EU/PT students.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Public licenciatura / mestrado integrado for PT/EU-equivalent students: usually up to about €697/year where the statutory cap applies. Mestrado and doutoramento tuition varies much more by institution and programme."
issues.source: Lei 37/2003 as amended by Lei 75/2019; DGES tuition guidance

issues.current: "label": "Bac state univ (ЕС)", "amountEURMin": 697, "amountEURMax": 1400
issues.problem: Same tuition-cap issue as in the cost section: the max of €1,400 for EU/public bachelor is misleading.
issues.suggestion: Change to approximately €697 max for capped public licenciatura, or add a note that exceptions/special programmes may differ and the exact fee is set in each institution's annual table.
issues.source: Lei 37/2003 as amended by Lei 75/2019

issues.current: Ограничение: доход семьи ≤ 5× IAS
issues.problem: The DGES social scholarship threshold is not simply 'family income ≤ 5×IAS'. Eligibility uses annual per-capita household income and other asset/academic criteria. With IAS 2026 = €537.13, quoting an unexplained 5×IAS is both outdated-looking and misleading.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Eligibility is formula-based: annual per-capita household income and assets are assessed under the Regulamento de Atribuição de Bolsas; in recent rules the key annual per-capita threshold is expressed as a multiple of IAS, plus movable-assets limits and ECTS/enrolment requirements. IAS 2026 = €537.13. Use the DGES/SAS simulator and annual regulation."
issues.source: DGES Regulamento de Atribuição de Bolsas de Estudo; IAS 2026 = €537.13

issues.current: **FCT (Fundação para a Ciência e Tecnologia)**: PhD bolsa 1100 €/мес + tuition
issues.problem: The FCT doctoral grant value is outdated. It has been above €1,100/month for several years; the domestic PhD grant value is around €1,259.64/month in recent FCT tables, with different values for mixed/abroad grants.
issues.suggestion: Write: "FCT PhD bolsa: check the annual FCT table; recent domestic doctoral grants are about €1,260/month plus eligible tuition/insurance components, with different values for mixed/abroad grants."
issues.source: FCT table of monthly maintenance allowances for research grants

issues.current: "label": "Bolsa FCT PhD", "amountEUR": 1100
issues.problem: Outdated FCT PhD monthly grant amount.
issues.suggestion: Update to the current FCT doctoral grant table value, or avoid a hard number and link to FCT's annual table. If using the recent domestic value, use about €1,259.64/month rather than €1,100.
issues.source: FCT table of monthly maintenance allowances for research grants

issues.current: **Erasmus+**: для резидентов / краткий обмен, 800-1000 €/мес
issues.problem: The amount is too high as a general statement for Erasmus+ study mobility from Portugal within Europe. Erasmus grants vary by destination and mobility type; many intra-European study grants are significantly below €800/month, with possible top-ups for fewer-opportunity students or travel/green travel.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Erasmus+: amount depends on destination group, mobility type and top-ups; intra-European study mobility is often a few hundred euros per month, while some international mobility/special cases can be higher. Check the university's Erasmus table for the year."
issues.source: Erasmus+ Programme Guide; Portuguese National Agency Erasmus+

issues.current: Erasmus+ — полный доступ.
issues.problem: Overbroad. Non-EU students enrolled in Portuguese institutions can often participate, but access depends on the institution's Erasmus charter, agreements, mobility type, nationality/residence rules, and scholarship budget. It is not an unconditional 'full access'.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Erasmus+: доступен через Portuguese HEI if the programme/institution has agreements and you meet mobility rules; funding is not guaranteed."
issues.source: Erasmus+ Programme Guide

issues.current: **Universidade Nova de Lisboa** — модерный, топ-300 QS.
issues.problem: Likely outdated/inaccurate for QS 2026: NOVA is not generally top-300 in the overall QS ranking; it is closer to top-400. Nova SBE may rank highly in specific business-school rankings, but that is a separate claim.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Universidade Nova de Lisboa — top-400 QS overall; Nova SBE is highly ranked in business/economics-specific rankings."
issues.source: QS World University Rankings 2026

issues.current: 🍽️ Столовая университета: 2.65 €/приём (cantina social)
issues.problem: This looks too specific and may be outdated. Social meal prices vary by SAS/institution and can change annually; many are around but not necessarily exactly €2.65.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Cantina social: usually around €2.50-€3.50 per meal depending on SAS/institution; check your university's Serviços de Ação Social."
issues.source: University Serviços de Ação Social price tables

issues.current: 🚇 Студенческий passe: 30 €/мес (Lisbon/Porto)
issues.problem: Incomplete for 2026. In Lisbon/Porto the standard municipal pass can be around €30, but many students under 23 are eligible for free or discounted public transport under the sub23/free pass regimes, subject to conditions.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Transport: standard Lisbon/Porto municipal passes are about €30/month, but many students under 23 can get free/discounted sub23 passes; rules depend on age, enrolment and operator/municipality."
issues.source: IMT/Área Metropolitana de Lisboa/Área Metropolitana do Porto sub23 pass rules

issues.current: 💳 Карта Acedo (студенческая): скидки на транспорт, еду, билеты
issues.problem: Likely typo/incorrect card name. There is no standard national 'Cartão Acedo' student card. Students normally use the university student card, ISIC, European Youth Card/Cartão Jovem, or specific transport sub23 documentation.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Student card / cartão de estudante, ISIC or Cartão Jovem: discounts vary; for transport use the relevant sub23/student pass documents."
issues.source: ISIC Portugal; Cartão Jovem; local transport operators

issues.current: Courses португальского для иностранцев бесплатно в большинстве универов
issues.problem: Overstated. Some universities offer free or subsidized Portuguese courses for enrolled international students, but many charge fees or have limited places.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Portuguese courses are offered by many universities; some are free for enrolled international students, others are paid or have limited places."
issues.source: University international office language-course pages

issues.current: Подача в консульстве страны happy
issues.problem: Text contains a typo/placeholder and is unclear. D4 applications are usually filed via the Portuguese consulate or outsourced visa center competent for the applicant's country of legal residence, not any country.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Подача через Portuguese consulate/VFS/BLS competent for your country of legal residence."
issues.source: Portal Diplomático / VFS Portugal visa application pages

issues.current: Или переход на D3 / D8 / D2 после получения контракта / бизнеса
issues.problem: Visa-code language is confusing inside Portugal. D2/D3/D8 are visa categories for entry from abroad; when already resident, the practical step is normally applying for/altering/renewing a residence permit under the relevant legal basis. D3 also requires highly qualified activity, not just any contract; D8 is remote-work/digital-nomad income; D2 is entrepreneurship/self-employment.
issues.suggestion: Write: "После учёбы можно менять legal basis of residence if conditions are met: work contract/subordinate work or highly qualified work, research/teaching, self-employment/entrepreneurship, or remote-work routes. D2/D3/D8 are visa labels mainly for entry applications; AIMA will assess the corresponding residence-permit basis."
issues.source: Lei 23/2007; AIMA residence permit categories

issues.current: https://www.dges.gov.pt/guias/indest.asp
issues.problem: This old DGES guide URL may still redirect/work, but it is a brittle legacy endpoint for a guide that changes annually.
issues.suggestion: Prefer linking to the current DGES CNA portal/Guia da Candidatura page for the relevant academic year, and mention that URLs change annually.
issues.source: DGES CNA portal

missing_topics: Official AIMA/Portal Diplomático source for D4 visa/residence requirements and current 2026 AIMA fee table, which changed on 01.03.2026
Clear explanation of the Estatuto do Estudante Internacional and the 2-year residence/equal-rights exceptions
CPLP caveat: some universities apply special CPLP tuition reductions or admission rules, but this is institutional and not the same as EU/PT fees
Recognition/equivalence path for foreign secondary education via DGE/Portuguese schools, distinct from DGES higher-degree recognition
Post-study residence route after graduation, distinct from the external job-seeker visa
Caveat on the nationality-law reform approved on 01.04.2026 and awaiting promulgation as of April 2026
DGES scholarship regulation details: per-capita income, assets, ECTS/enrolment requirements, deadlines and simulator
Whether a programme is accredited by A3ES/DGES and how to verify accreditation before paying tuition
Health coverage nuance for non-EU students: private insurance for visa/residence, then possible SNS registration depending on residence status and NISS/NIF/local rules
